Renewed Motion By Plaintiff And Cross-Defendant Phunware, Inc. And Third Party Defendant Fetch Media, Ltd. To Stay This Action Pending Resolution Of Fetchs Declaratory Judgment Action In Federal Court
Off Calendar Feb-07-2018 Continued To Feb-28-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF PHUNWARE, INC. and CROSS DEFENDANT FETCH MEDIA, LTD.'S Renewed Motion To Stay This Action Pending Resolution Of Fetch's Declaratory Judgment Action In Federal Court is CONTINUED to February 28, 2018 to give the federal court the opportunity to issue a ruling on Uber's motion to dismiss or stay the federal case. Based on comity, this court is pleased to proceed in any manner that the federal court believes is most appropriate. If the federal court believes that the federal action should proceed, then this court will stay this action pending the conclusion of the federal action. If the federal court believes that the federal action should be dismissed or stayed, this court will deny the motion to stay this case. If the federal court is interested in the views of this court, this court has the tentative view that the factors considered in Caiafa Prof. Law Corp. v. State Farm Fire & Cas. Co. (1993) 15 Cal.App.4th 800 do not weigh in favor of granting the motion to stay this case.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: torreanom@aol.com; mtorreano@sftc.org.
